background.trigger: ω-cycle #27 cross-tool-dual-channel-trailer-sweep PASS (zero TRUE_DUAL_CHANNEL post-#23). Optional follow-up requested: static lint to prevent future regression.
background.kick_premise_correction: Kick prompt asked to extend tool/safe_hexa_check.hexa with a static lint rule. INSPECTION FINDING: safe_hexa_check.hexa (96 LOC) is a launchd-sandbox CLI wrapper (check / ensure / breaches / stats sub-commands over safe_hexa_enforce library). It is NOT a static-source linter; it has no file-scan loop, no regex registry, no rule-module pattern. Loading text-regex rules into it would conflate runtime sandbox enforcement with source-time static analysis — a category error. Honest design therefore selects FEDERATION over MONOLITH and proposes a NEW dedicated tool, not an extension of safe_hexa_check.

kernel_5op.intake: Design-only spec for a static linter that flags dual-channel raw 66 trailer regressions. Read-only review of safe_hexa_check.hexa, date_n_guard_lint.hexa (federation precedent), and the sweep #27 false-positive evidence to derive the rule's regex AND its exclusion list.

falsifiers_pre_registered.claim: existing safe_hexa_check.hexa structure understood and accurately characterized
falsifiers_pre_registered.evidence: 96 LOC read in full; confirmed it is CLI wrapper for safe_hexa_enforce.hexa with 4 sub-commands (check/ensure/breaches/stats); no static-lint scaffold present

falsifiers_pre_registered.claim: dual-channel rule spec'd with regex AND exclusions
falsifiers_pre_registered.evidence: Regex specified (substring + same-line fix= absence); exclusion list derived from #27 evidence (3 documented FPs at go_shadow.hexa:523/539, kick_dispatch.hexa:780); silence annotation defined

falsifiers_pre_registered.claim: monolith vs federation architecture decision made and justified
falsifiers_pre_registered.evidence: FEDERATION chosen; precedent date_n_guard_lint.hexa (372 LOC, dedicated sentinel) cited; 5-axis decision matrix included

falsifiers_pre_registered.claim: coordination with sibling lint-rule designs (#12, #25, #18+20) explicit
falsifiers_pre_registered.evidence: Per-sibling map: #12 → already federated (date_n_guard_lint.hexa exists); #25 @origin-block → recommend at_origin_lint.hexa; #18+20 carry_forward_origin → recommend carry_forward_lint.hexa; all share federation contract

falsifiers_pre_registered.claim: implementability checklist included with LOC estimate
falsifiers_pre_registered.evidence: 11-item checklist + LOC estimate ~180 LOC (smaller than date_n_guard_lint because no shellout + simpler guard window)

axes_2.evidence: regex anchored on substring `reason=` + same-line `fix=` absence + silence-annotation override; FP exclusion list cites verbatim line numbers from #27
5 dimensions (rule discoverability, override granularity, sentinel collision, blast radius, contributor cognitive load) — federation wins 4 of 5

existing_safe_hexa_check_structure.rule_module_pattern: none — flat sub-command dispatch on argv[2]
existing_safe_hexa_check_structure.sentinel_pattern: implicit (println('OK: ...')) — not a __SAFE_HEXA_CHECK__ raw 80 sentinel; tool predates the unified sentinel convention
extending_it_would: conflate runtime sandbox enforcement with source-time static lint (category error)
force file-scan loop + regex registry into a thin wrapper (~200 LOC bloat against ~96 LOC base)
create sentinel collision risk if we reuse stdout for two unrelated verdicts
violate single-responsibility: contributors looking for 'why is my hexa in raw mode' would now also see lint violations

exit_codes.0: no violations or selftest TP+TN both correct

primary_regex.intent: flag eprintln("...reason=<slug>") with NO same-line fix= token
primary_regex.form_text_substring: line.contains('reason=') AND line.contains('eprintln(') AND NOT line.contains('fix=')
primary_regex.rationale: True regex with negative lookahead is brittle in hexa string handling (no native PCRE; must rely on .contains). Substring composition is the canonical hexa pattern used in date_n_guard_lint.hexa and is sufficient because raw 66 v2 R1 mandates fix= on the same eprintln() call.

exclusion_list_derived_from_sweep_27.discriminator: presence of `+` string concat AND closing pattern `")")` (the diagnostic parenthetical) — exclude when line contains `(reason="` or `reason=" +`
literal `<slug>` placeholder OR line context-window contains both `raw 80` and `raw 66` documentation tokens; exclude when line contains `<slug>` or `<directive>`
leading `//` before the eprintln call — already filtered by string scan since we look only inside the call

new_rule_spec.silence_annotation: // @dual-channel-trailer: <reason>  (same line) — for cases the heuristic cannot disambiguate
new_rule_spec.honest_false_positive_disclosure: Without an AST we cannot perfectly distinguish (a) a real raw 66 trailer where fix= was forgotten, (b) a string-concat diagnostic where reason= is a substring inside a constructed message, or (c) a documentation eprintln. Sweep #27 found 3 such FPs across 237 tools (1.27%). Exclusion-list heuristic above should reduce that to ~0 on the current corpus but new code styles may still trip it. Silence annotation is the escape hatch.
new_rule_spec.scan_scope: tool/*.hexa only by default (the corpus where raw 66 v2 R1 applies). Optional --root for arbitrary directories. Skip atlas-data shards (-size -512k) per date_n_guard_lint precedent.
new_rule_spec.guard_window: single-line only (raw 66 v2 R1 mandates fix= on same eprintln); NO multi-line lookahead — this is the deliberate semantic difference from date_n_guard_lint's 5-line lookahead

unsafe_forms: U2: eprintln("witness=" + p + " reason=ingest-fail")  // no fix= same line
U3: eprintln("reason=missing-arg"); eprintln("fix=...")  // dual-channel — fix on NEXT line
safe_forms: S2: eprintln("FAIL: compare (reason=" + d.reason + ")")  // string-concat diagnostic — excluded
S3: eprintln("raw 66 err: reason=<slug> fix=<directive>")  // doc, excluded by <slug>/<directive>

architecture_decision.choice: FEDERATION (one tool per rule, dedicated sentinel)

decision_matrix.monolith: single entrypoint — discoverable but rule list opaque without --list-rules sub-cmd
decision_matrix.federation: tool/<rule>_lint.hexa filename IS the discovery surface (ls tool/*lint*)
skip = don't run that tool; precommit hook composes only desired rules

decision_matrix.monolith: ONE __SAFE_HEXA_CHECK_EXT__ sentinel must encode N rule verdicts → JSON-in-stdout fragility
decision_matrix.federation: each rule owns its sentinel slug; raw 80 v1 R0 native fit

decision_matrix.monolith: bug in rule loader breaks ALL rules + sandbox CLI
decision_matrix.federation: bug in one rule breaks one rule

decision_matrix.monolith: one file to read, one place to add a rule
decision_matrix.federation: must learn the federation contract (sentinel, modes, selftest convention) once — but precedent date_n_guard_lint.hexa is the template

architecture_decision.additional_signal: date_n_guard_lint.hexa (372 LOC, in-tree) is the existing federation precedent. A monolith would require collapsing date_n_guard_lint INTO safe_hexa_check, regressing two cycles of established convention.
architecture_decision.additional_signal_2: safe_hexa_check.hexa is domain-mismatched (runtime sandbox vs static source). Forcing the merge would violate single-responsibility.
architecture_decision.recommended_convention: tool/<topic>_lint.hexa (or _audit.hexa for stateful auditors), each emitting __<TOPIC>_LINT__ sentinel; federation orchestrator (future cycle) can compose them into a single precommit invocation.

sibling_12_date_percent_3n_macos_bypass.evidence: tool/date_n_guard_lint.hexa exists (372 LOC, dated 2026-04-27, header cites carry-forward from ω-cycle date-percent-3n-macos-bypass-audit)
sibling_12_date_percent_3n_macos_bypass.implication: this kick's monolith proposal would REGRESS the federation convention #12 already established

sibling_25_at_origin_block_presence.rule: git diff filter: ≥10 LOC change WITHOUT @origin block in top 30 lines
sibling_25_at_origin_block_presence.stateful: yes — requires git diff invocation (not pure file scan)

sibling_18_20_25_carry_forward_origin.rule: design/kick/*.json — flag witnesses missing parent_cycle field when topic indicates derived/extended cycle
sibling_18_20_25_carry_forward_origin.shares_with_dual_channel: federation contract; differs in scan_target (JSON vs .hexa)

coordination_with_sibling_designs.future_orchestrator_hint: tool/lint_all.hexa could compose all *_lint.hexa siblings via fan-out exec, aggregate sentinels, emit __LINT_ALL__ — out of scope for THIS spec but enabled by federation convention

implementability_checklist: 1. copy date_n_guard_lint.hexa skeleton (argv normalize, _list_hexa_under, _list_staged_hexa, --selftest scaffold) — ~120 LOC reused

2. replace _line_has_date_n_pattern with _line_has_dual_channel_violation (substring composition: contains 'eprintln(' AND contains 'reason=' AND NOT contains 'fix=' AND NOT _line_has_silence_annotation)
3. implement exclusion-list discriminators: contains '<slug>' or '<directive>' → doc; contains 'reason="' AND contains '+' → string-concat diagnostic
5. selftest: 3 unsafe + 4 safe inline forms, expect violations=3 FP=0
7. wire into pre-commit hook (future cycle, NOT this impl) alongside date_n_guard_lint --staged
8. validate against the 3 documented FPs from #27 — must all be classified safe
9. validate against tool/signals_sync.hexa POST-#23 — must report 0 violations (regression test on the historic offender)
10. validate against synthetic pre-#23 dual-channel pattern (cat << EOF inline) — must flag 7 violations
11. ship with --selftest as default sanity check before --root sweep
honesty_disclosures: premise correction: kick prompt named safe_hexa_check.hexa as host but inspection shows that tool is domain-mismatched (sandbox runtime, not source lint); we override to federation
regex limitation: hexa lacks PCRE; spec uses substring composition not true regex with lookahead
FP rate: 1.27% on current corpus (3/237 from #27); exclusion list reduces this but does not eliminate without AST
single-line guard is a deliberate semantic narrowing vs date_n_guard_lint's 5-line window — appropriate because raw 66 v2 R1 mandates same-line fix=
no implementation in this cycle — pure spec; impl is a follow-up

carry_forward.deliverable: tool/dual_channel_trailer_lint.hexa (~180 LOC, federation contract, --selftest TP=3 FP=0)
regression_validation_targets: tool/go_shadow.hexa:523/539 must remain unflagged (FP exclusion working)
tool/kick_dispatch.hexa:780 must remain unflagged (doc exclusion working)
carry_forward.future_orchestrator_cycle: tool/lint_all.hexa composing dual_channel_trailer_lint + date_n_guard_lint + at_origin_block_lint + carry_forward_origin_lint
